content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 72,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 45,
    "interaction_quality": 68,
    "ai_integration": 74,
    "mobile_first": 52,
    "performance": 60,
    "typescript_quality": 70,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 62,
    "accessibility": 38,
    "security": 58,
    "differentiation": 70,
    "enterprise_readiness": 48
  },
  "overall_score": 59,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C5-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Dashboard page missing real metric cards with computed project KPIs",
      "file": "src/pages/Dashboard.tsx",
      "description": "The Dashboard page acceptance criteria require 6-8 computed metric cards (Schedule Health, Budget Health, RFI Status, Punch List, Safety, Financial) with trend indicators, severity colors, and clickable navigation. Currently the dashboard likely renders placeholder or mock data cards without real Supabase queries computing open RFI counts, overdue counts, budget variance, punch completion percentages, or days since last incident. This is the #1 page a superintendent opens at 6 AM.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Dashboard.tsx, in the main Dashboard component: Add a useEffect or React Query hook that fetches real aggregated metrics from Supabase in a single batched query. BEFORE: Metric cards show hardcoded or mock values like '12 Open RFIs' without querying the database. AFTER: On mount, query rfis table for open/overdue counts, punch_list_items for open/completed/verified counts, budget_items for total budget vs spent vs variance, daily_logs for latest safety incident count, and schedule_phases for on-track percentage. Each MetricBox receives computed values with trend arrows (compare last 30 days). Numbers use compact formatting ($1.2M). Each card is clickable and navigates to the detail page. Empty project state shows onboarding checklist. Add skeleton loaders matching the 2x3 grid layout while loading. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C5-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "CopilotPanel and AgentMentionInput missing ARIA roles and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The CopilotPanel slide-in panel lacks role='dialog', aria-modal, aria-label, and focus trapping. The message list has no aria-live region for new messages. The agent mention dropdown in AgentMentionInput lacks listbox/option roles. Keyboard users cannot navigate the suggested prompts or close the panel with Escape. This fails WCAG 2.1 AA and the global acceptance criteria for accessibility.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: BEFORE: The panel's root aside element has no ARIA attributes. Messages are not announced to screen readers. Escape key does not close the panel. Suggested prompt buttons lack aria-label. AFTER: Add role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-label='AI Copilot' to the aside element. Add an onKeyDown handler that calls closeCopilot on Escape. Wrap the messages container in a div with aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions' so new AI messages are announced. Add aria-label to each suggested prompt button describing its action. Add tabIndex={0} to the first focusable element and auto-focus it when the panel opens using a useEffect with a ref. Ensure the close button has aria-label='Close AI Copilot'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C5-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "AI Copilot lacks conversation persistence and history retrieval from Supabase",
      "file": "src/hooks/useMultiAgentChat.ts",
      "description": "The AI Copilot chat does not persist conversations to Supabase ai_conversations and ai_messages tables. When a user refreshes the page or returns later, all conversation history is lost. The PRODUCTION_ROADMAP and backend architecture specify these tables exist. Enterprise users need audit trails of AI interactions and the ability to reference prior AI analysis. Without persistence, the copilot is a toy, not a tool.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useMultiAgentChat.ts, in the useMultiAgentChat hook: BEFORE: Messages are stored only in local React state. No Supabase writes occur. Page refresh loses all conversation history. AFTER: On first user message, create a row in ai_conversations table with project_id, user_id, conversation_topic derived from the first message (truncated to 50 chars), and started_at. Store the conversation_id in state. On each message (user or assistant), insert into ai_messages table with conversation_id, role, content, and metadata containing token count if available. On hook mount, accept an optional conversationId prop. If provided, load existing messages from ai_messages ordered by created_at ascending and populate the messages state. Add a useConversationHistory function that queries ai_conversations for the current project ordered by started_at desc, limit 20, so the UI can show recent conversations. Handle Supabase errors gracefully with console.warn, never blocking the chat flow. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C5-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "FloatingAIButton and CopilotPanel not optimized for mobile field use",
      "file": "src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx",
      "description": "The FloatingAIButton uses a fixed 48px button that may overlap mobile bottom navigation. The CopilotPanel opens as a 360px side panel which on phones under 375px width either overflows or becomes unusable. Touch targets for suggested prompts are too small. The acceptance criteria require bottom sheet modals on mobile and 44x44px minimum touch targets. Field workers need the copilot accessible with one hand on a phone.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: BEFORE: The button has a fixed bottom/right position that may conflict with mobile bottom nav. The isMobile check adjusts spacing but the button size stays at spacing['12'] (48px). No safe area inset handling. AFTER: On mobile (width < 768px), position the button at bottom: 'calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 72px)' to account for bottom nav and safe areas. Increase the touch target to 56px on mobile (width and height). Add a subtle scale transform on active state (transform: 'scale(0.95)') for tactile feedback. Ensure the button has a minimum 44x44px touch target per Apple HIG. Add a CSS media query via inline style that sets the z-index above any bottom navigation (z-index 1100). The aria-label should include the insight count for screen readers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C5-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "AI insights API endpoint swallows errors and falls back to stale placeholder data silently",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/ai.ts",
      "description": "In getAiInsights, when both the AI service and Supabase cached insights return empty, the function generates placeholder 'onboarding' insights that look like real data but are fabricated. There is no visual indicator to the user that insights are synthetic. The catch block for aiService.generateInsights logs a warning but the UI has no way to know the data source degraded. Users making decisions based on stale or placeholder insights is a trust killer for enterprise adoption.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function: BEFORE: When cachedInsights.length === 0 and budgetInsights.length === 0, the function generates placeholder insights with hardcoded titles like 'Welcome to SiteSync AI' without any indicator they are synthetic. The catch block for aiService failure silently falls back. AFTER: When generating placeholder/onboarding insights, add a field source: 'onboarding' to each insight object (distinct from 'live' and 'cached'). In the response, include a degraded: true field when the AI service failed and we fell back to cache or placeholders. Change the dataSource to 'ai-degraded' when the AI service threw an error. Remove the silent console.warn and instead include the error type in the response metadata so the frontend can show a subtle banner: 'AI insights may be delayed. Showing cached analysis.' Never generate fake metric values in placeholders; only show instructional/onboarding content. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Morning Project Pulse",
      "description": "Automatically generated one-paragraph natural language summary sent to PM and superintendent at 6 AM. Combines overnight weather changes, RFI status shifts, schedule risk from AI analysis, and yesterday's daily log highlights. Delivered as both an in-app notification card on the Dashboard and an optional email digest. This is the 'Project Pulse' feature from the founder's wish list — it makes the Dashboard the first thing opened every morning."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's AI Assist is a search-over-docs chatbot that cannot take actions or render interactive UI. SiteSync's multi-agent architecture with generative UI (charts, approval cards, data tables rendered inline) is a genuine differentiator. However, Procore's Agent Builder allows no-code AI agents for drafting RFIs and generating daily summaries — features SiteSync has architecturally but hasn't wired to real data. The biggest gap vs. Procore is that SiteSync's copilot conversations don't persist and insights degrade to placeholders silently, which would erode trust in a demo. Autodesk Construction IQ provides predictive risk scoring from real project data — SiteSync's predictive alerts currently use mock data. Fixing data richness and persistence would put SiteSync ahead of both competitors on AI depth.",
  "summary": "The Project Intelligence module has an impressive architectural foundation: multi-agent chat with generative UI components, specialist agent routing, batch action previews, and a rich component library (13+ generative UI renderers). The differentiation score is strong because no competitor ships inline interactive charts, approval cards, and data tables from AI responses. However, the module is hollow — conversations don't persist to Supabase, dashboard metrics aren't computed from real data, AI insights silently degrade to placeholders, accessibility is poor (no ARIA on the copilot panel), and mobile field use is compromised by small touch targets and desktop-first panel sizing. The five fixes target: (1) real computed dashboard KPIs, (2) copilot ARIA and keyboard access, (3) conversation persistence to ai_conversations/ai_messages, (4) mobile-optimized FAB and panel, and (5) honest error handling that never shows fake insights as real. These changes would move the score from 59 to approximately 72 by establishing trust in the data layer and making the AI copilot usable for field crews."
}
```
